title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to modify the railroad track maintenance credit.
plain_summary: This bill changes the tax credit that railroad companies can get for maintaining their train tracks. Right now, companies can get up to $3,500 per mile of track they maintain, but this bill increases that amount to $6,100 per mile. Starting in 2026, this credit amount will go up each year to keep up with inflation. The bill also lets railroad companies claim the credit for tracks they got as late as 2024, instead of only tracks they had by 2015. These changes will start in 2025.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Modification of Railroad Track Maintenance Credit
sections.text: This section changes the tax credit that railroad companies can claim for maintaining their tracks. First, it increases the credit amount from three thousand five hundred dollars per mile to six thousand one hundred dollars per mile. Second, it adds a new rule that automatically adjusts this amount each year for inflation starting in twenty twenty six. The adjustment will be based on the cost of living changes compared to twenty twenty four. If the inflation adjustment results in an amount that is not a round hundred dollars, it will be rounded to the nearest hundred dollars. Third, it updates the date for what counts as qualified track maintenance spending from January first twenty fifteen to January first twenty twenty four. All of these changes apply to money spent on track maintenance in tax years that begin after December thirty first twenty twenty four.
